stem: A guy wire 34 feet long supports a vertical antenna tower. The wire is anchored to the ground at a point 16 feet from the base of the tower. What is the height, in feet, of the point where the guy wire attaches to the tower?
explanation: The guy wire (34 feet) is the hypotenuse and the ground distance (16 feet) is one leg of the right triangle. Using the Pythagorean theorem to find the other leg: height = √(34² − 16²) = √(1156 − 256) = √900 = 30 feet. This uses the 8-15-17 Pythagorean triple scaled by 2.

distractorRationale.B: Added the wire length and ground distance instead of using the Pythagorean theorem, computing 34 + 16 = 50.
distractorRationale.C: Subtracted the ground distance from the wire length instead of using the Pythagorean theorem, computing 34 − 16 = 18.
distractorRationale.D: Averaged the wire length and ground distance instead of using the Pythagorean theorem, computing (34 + 16) ÷ 2 = 25.
